digest.top_line: TREASURE: consolidative thoracic RT on an atezo-maintenance backbone in ES-SCLC halted early for toxicity; mOS 6.7 vs 13.4mo (HR 1.55, ns), SAEs 61% vs 18%.
digest.tldr: Thoracic carried the day's only signal, and it's a negative one for RT: TREASURE shows consolidative TRT (30 Gy/10) on an atezolizumab-maintenance backbone adds no OS in unselected ES-SCLC while tripling SAEs, driven by lymphopenia-linked infections. The CREST-era benefit of thoracic RT doesn't transfer onto an IO backbone.

sites.intro: One study, and it argues against adding consolidative thoracic RT to IO maintenance in unselected ES-SCLC.

studies.name: TREASURE
studies.tldr: mOS 6.7 vs 13.4mo (HR 1.55, ns) adding consolidative TRT to atezo maintenance; halted early for SAEs 61% vs 18%.
details: 🔍 Phase 2 open-label RCT (AIO-TRK-0320), 20 sites Germany/Austria; 68 randomized (34/arm) of planned 104, stopped early by SMC for fatal SAEs in arm A
🔍 ES-SCLC with ≥stable disease after induction carboplatin-etoposide-atezolizumab; consolidative TRT = 30 Gy in 10 fractions added to atezo maintenance
⚠️ Underpowered by early stop: design needed 92 pts / 66 events for 80% power (assumed HR 0.57); OS HR 1.55 is not a reliable efficacy estimate
⚠️ Mechanism: persistent lymphocyte depletion after TRT drove infection and respiratory SAEs; pts with fatal AEs in arm A had lower baseline DLCO
🔗 vs CREST (pre-immunotherapy): modest OS benefit from thoracic RT after chemo; TREASURE finds that benefit does not transfer onto an IO-maintenance backbone
⚠️ Unselected population, no lung-function gating; authors argue against routine use, RT only in carefully selected pts

verdict.rationale: Randomised harm signal (SAEs 61% vs 18%, fatal 19% vs 3%) argues against adding consolidative TRT to IO maintenance, contesting emerging RT enthusiasm; OS worse but underpowered by early stop.
verdict.audience: ES-SCLC, ≥stable disease after chemo-IO induction; unselected

studies.significance: The harm, not the null OS, is the RT read: SAEs 61% vs 18%, fatal AEs 19% vs 3%, driven by post-TRT lymphocyte depletion and low baseline DLCO in the fatal cases. 30Gy/10fx consolidative TRT on atezo maintenance is net harmful in unselected ES-SCLC; any future use needs lung-function gating.
studies.monday_clinic: In unselected ES-SCLC with at least stable disease after chemo-IO induction, this evidence argues against routinely adding consolidative thoracic RT to atezolizumab maintenance; it does not address the good-lung-function subset the authors flag as possibly selectable.

primary_endpoint.name: Overall survival
primary_endpoint.stat_detail: HR 1.55 (95% CI 0.90-2.69), P=.34; TRT arm worse, ns

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Phase 2 open-label RCT (AIO-TRK-0320), 1:1, 20 sites in Germany/Austria. Planned 104 pts; **halted early by the SMC after 68 randomized** (34/arm) for excess fatal SAEs in the TRT arm. Recruited 2020-2022, last follow-up Sept 2024, post-hoc survival update April 2026.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: ES-SCLC with **at least stable disease after induction carboplatin-etoposide-atezolizumab**. Baseline well balanced between arms and between pts with vs without SAEs. **Unselected for lung function.**

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: **Consolidative TRT 30 Gy in 10 fractions** added to atezolizumab maintenance (arm A) vs atezolizumab maintenance alone (arm B).

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: **Primary: overall survival.** Secondary included PFS and safety.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: Primary OS **not met**: the TRT arm was numerically worse with no PFS difference and sharply higher serious and fatal toxicity (see table).

analysis_sections.label: Safety
analysis_sections.body: SAEs dominated by **infection and respiratory disorders**, linked to **radiation-induced lymphocyte depletion**; fatal-AE pts in arm A had **lower baseline DLCO**. No other risk factors identified.

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: **CREST** (pre-immunotherapy) showed a modest OS benefit from thoracic RT after chemo; TREASURE tested that strategy on an **IMpower133-style IO-maintenance backbone** and found harm, not benefit.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents unselected ES-SCLC pts with at least stable disease after chemo-immunotherapy induction. Does not represent lung-function-selected pts or those with poor baseline DLCO, in whom TRT was not separately tested.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Open-label; **small N (68) and early termination** leave OS underpowered with a wide CI. Phase 2; efficacy conclusions provisional, though the safety signal is robust.

open_questions: Which lung-function-selected ES-SCLC pts might tolerate consolidative TRT on IO maintenance
Whether lower-dose or delayed TRT lessens lymphocyte depletion and infection risk

related_trials.brief_title: Adebrelimab Combined with Chemotherapy and Thoracic Radiotherapy for First-line Treatment of ES-SCLC
related_trials.brief_summary: This phase III trial compares the effect of adding radiation therapy to the usual maintenance therapy with adebrelimab versus adebrelimab alone in patients who have already received debrelimab plus chemotherapy for the treatment of extensive stage small cell lung cancer.
conditions: Extensive Stage Lung Small Cell Carcinoma
related_trials.answers_question: Which lung-function-selected ES-SCLC pts might tolerate consolidative TRT on IO maintenance
related_trials.relevance_phrase: phase 3 consolidative TRT on adebrelimab IO maintenance

papers.title: Consolidative Thoracic Radiotherapy With Atezolizumab Maintenance in Extensive-Stage Small Cell Lung Cancer
papers.abstract: Importance Chemoimmunotherapy followed by immunotherapy maintenance is the standard first-line treatment for extensive-stage small cell lung cancer (ES-SCLC), yet data regarding efficacy and safety of consolidative thoracic radiotherapy (TRT) are lacking. Objective To determine whether combining consolidative TRT with immunotherapy maintenance in ES-SCLC is safe and improves patients’ overall survival (OS) and progression-free survival (PFS). Design, Settings, and Participants This was a multicenter open-label phase 2 randomized clinical trial recruiting patients from September 2020 to August 2022 in Germany and Austria, with the last follow-up visit in September 2024. Eligible patients had ES-SCLC with at least stable disease after induction chemoimmunotherapy (carboplatin−etoposide−atezolizumab). Although the database was locked in April 2025, a post hoc survival update was performed in April 2026. Data were analyzed from April 2025 to April 2026. Interventions Randomized (1:1) to either atezolizumab maintenance combined with consolidative TRT (30 Gy in 10 fractions) (arm A) or atezolizumab maintenance alone (arm B). Main Outcome and Measure OS (time from randomization to death due to any cause). Results Of 96 patients assessed for eligibility, 68 patients were randomized; recruitment was prematurely terminated due to safety concerns. Median OS in the combination arm was numerically shorter compared to atezolizumab only (6.7 months [95% CI, 5.1-9.0] vs 13.4 months [95% CI, 10.7-17.5]; HR, 1.55 [95% CI, 0.90-2.69]; P = .34), while median PFS was similar (2.4 months [95% CI, 1.3-3.9] vs 2.6 months [95% CI, 1.2-3.9]; HR, 0.92 [95% CI, 0.54-1.55]; P = .85). TRT plus atezolizumab was accompanied by higher frequency of severe adverse events (SAEs) (19 patients [61.3%] vs 6 patients [18.2%]; P &amp;lt; .001) and fatal outcomes (6 patients [19.4%] vs 1 patient [3.0%]; P = .04). Safety analysis revealed predominance of infection and respiratory disorder−related SAEs, possibly facilitated by a depletion of lymphocytes observed specifically in patients after TRT, and by a lower single breath diffuse capacity of the lungs for carbon monoxide in patients with fatal AEs in arm A. No further risk factors were identified, given that baseline characteristics were well balanced between both arms and between patients with and without SAEs, including fatal SAEs. Conclusions and Relevance In this randomized clinical trial, TRT combined with immunotherapy increased toxic effects in unselected patients with ES-SCLC without survival benefit, presumably due to radiation-induced lymphocyte depletion facilitating infections. Cautious patient selection and further investigation to identify those who may benefit without undue risk are required. Trial Registration ClinicalTrials.gov Identifier: NCT04462276
